name: El Embalse de La Pedrera — Circular desde Almoradí
region_intro: Mountain biking around Almoradí features diverse landscapes within the Vega Baja region of Alicante, Spain. The area is characterized by an extensive network of trails, including paths along irrigation canals and the Segura River. Riders can explore agricultural lands, natural parks like El Hondo, and scenic reservoir areas such as La Pedrera. This varied terrain provides options for different skill levels, from gentle routes to more challenging mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Almoradí The most popular mountain bike trail is El Embalse de La Pedrera — Circular desde Almoradí, a 29.9 miles (48.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic La Pedrera Reservoir area. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Almoradí, a moderate 32.6 miles (52.4 km) path. This route offers views of the reservoir and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. Local mountain bikers also love the La Mata Lagoon – Stone Bridge of Rojales loop from Algorfa, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through picturesque natural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Almoradí is defined by irrigation canals, the Segura River, and natural parks, with routes offering options for various ability levels. The routes in Almoradí are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almoradí's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I expect around Almoradí?
A: Almoradí offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails suitable for various skill levels. You'll find routes that traverse agricultural lands, follow irrigation canals, and explore natural parks and reservoir areas. The terrain includes both easy, mostly paved sections and more challenging unpaved segments with significant elevation changes. Overall, there are over 30 mountain bike tours available, with a good mix of easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Almoradí?
A: Yes, Almoradí has several mountain bike trails that are suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes often feature mostly paved surfaces and lower elevation gains, making them accessible for all fitness levels. There are 5 easy mountain bike tours in the area.

Q: What about more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Almoradí offers routes with more significant elevation gains and technical sections. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14628891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezo de los Mozos – MTB trail loop from Almoradí&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route covering 36.5 km with over 630 meters of elevation gain, requiring good fitness. In total, there are 5 difficult mountain bike tours and 24 moderate ones that will test your skills.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Almoradí?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Almoradí are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930110573/el-embalse-de-la-pedrera-circular-desde-almoradi" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Embalse de La Pedrera — Circular desde Almoradí&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 48.1 km moderate trail exploring the scenic La Pedrera Reservoir area.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Almoradí?
A: Almoradí benefits from an excellent climate with an average of 300 sunny days a year, making it suitable for mountain biking almost year-round. However, it's advisable to avoid the hottest hours during the summer months. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities.

Q: What natural features or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Mountain bike trails in Almoradí often pass through beautiful natural landscapes. You can explore areas like the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1828095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and the extensive agricultural lands surrounding the Segura River. The nearby El Hondo Natural Park is renowned for its diverse birdlife, and you might even spot species from observatories like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Anguilla Observatory&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that follow the Segura River?
A: Yes, the Segura River and its surrounding 'huerta' (orchards and farmland) are prominent features in the region, offering picturesque routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40377420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mouth of the Segura River – Segura River Bridge loop from Almoradí&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 43.6 km mountain bike route that allows you to explore this area.

Q: Can I find mountain bike routes near La Mata Lagoon?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33774873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon – Stone Bridge of Rojales loop from Algorfa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 41.0 km trail that leads through the picturesque natural areas around La Mata Lagoon, offering great views and opportunities for nature observation.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Almoradí?
A: The mountain bike trails in Almoradí are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.0 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 250 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and the well-maintained network of trails.

Q: Are there any mountain bike routes that specifically explore the La Pedrera Reservoir area?
A: Yes, the La Pedrera Reservoir is a significant highlight for mountain bikers in the region. A popular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6891767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Almoradí&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 52.4 km path that offers extensive views of the reservoir and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills.
tips: For me it was the end of a very dry route. Although the views with the reservoir full are worth it.
